title: Prosci ADKAR Assessment
description: Schema for an ADKAR assessment based on Prosci's ADKAR Model, measuring individual or group change readiness across five sequential dimensions: Awareness, Desire, Knowledge, Ability, and Reinforcement.

projectId.description: Identifier of the associated change project

stakeholderId.description: Identifier of the assessed stakeholder or group

stakeholderName.description: Name of the assessed stakeholder or group

awareness.description: Awareness of the need for change - understanding why the change is necessary

desire.description: Desire to participate and support the change

knowledge.description: Knowledge of how to change - understanding required skills and behaviors

ability.description: Ability to implement required skills and behaviors on a day-to-day basis

reinforcement.description: Reinforcement to sustain the change and prevent regression

overallScore.description: Average score across all five ADKAR dimensions

barrierPoint.description: The first ADKAR element scoring below the threshold (typically 3), indicating the primary barrier to change adoption. The ADKAR model is sequential, so barriers must be resolved in order.

notes.description: Additional notes or observations from the assessor

assessedBy.description: Name or identifier of the person who conducted the assessment

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the assessment was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the assessment was last updated

adkarDimension.title: ADKAR Dimension
adkarDimension.description: Score and qualitative details for a single ADKAR dimension

score.description: Numeric score from 1 (low readiness) to 5 (high readiness). Scores of 3 or below typically indicate a barrier point requiring targeted intervention.

actions.description: Recommended corrective actions to improve this dimension

evidence.description: Observable evidence supporting the assigned score
